headline: Cyril Imhof's shorts converge on Anthropic's enterprise push, framing Claude's integration into Slack and Teams as the cycle's dominant thread.
body: Across a run of shorts, Cyril Imhof tracks Anthropic embedding Claude into productivity platforms like Slack and Microsoft Teams, questioning whether it is a deliberate European enterprise adoption strategy and whether Microsoft's ecosystem and its own Copilot can defend against direct integration [5][8][9][11]. He extends the argument to MCPs as a client-retention and revenue mechanism, and cites Claude Tag as accounting for 60% of interaction patterns inside Anthropic [6][7][13]. Cole Medin adds a positive take on Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5, calling it impressive for the cost [2], while Imhof separately argues open-source models are closing the gap on frontier systems from annual to monthly intervals and that Chinese models win on cost and data sovereignty [12][10]. Better Stack steps outside the Anthropic thread to test Kestra, a declarative YAML orchestration tool pitched as a lighter alternative to Apache Airflow [0].
